message:
🐛 Fix 12 missions with unreachable Helm repo URLs (#1470)

* 🐛 Fix 12 missions with unreachable Helm repo URLs

Validated all 107 Helm repo URLs against live servers. Fixed 12 missions:
- aeraki-mesh: no Helm chart exists → use install script from repo
- bank-vaults: bank-vaults.dev/charts 404 → use chart from vault-operator repo
- kagent: no published Helm repo → use local chart from cloned repo
- kaito: wrong path /kaito/charts → correct path /kaito
- kmesh: no published Helm repo → use deploy/charts from repo
- kubeedge: no Helm chart → use keadm CLI from releases
- kubefleet: no published Helm repo → use charts/ from repo
- wasmcloud: no published Helm repo → use charts/runtime-operator from repo
- containerssh: no Helm chart → use binary/Docker install
- tikv: charts.pingcap.org unreachable → use chart from tidb-operator repo
- pipecd: charts.pipecd.dev unreachable → use manifests from repo
- interlink: step 2 missing code block → added provider config example

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Properly rewrite tikv and pipecd missions (fix leftover helm repo URLs)

Previous fix left broken helm repo add commands pointing to
raw.githubusercontent.com (tikv) and charts.pipecd.dev (pipecd).
Rewrote both missions to use local charts from cloned repos.

Validated all 181 missions: 0 structural failures, 0 Helm repo 404s.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+        "description": "Each provider (SLURM, HTCondor, Docker, etc.) requires a sidecar configuration.

See the provider-specific docs:
```bash
# List available provider examples
ls -la /etc/interlink/

# For SLURM provider, configure the endpoint
cat > /etc/interlink/provider.yaml << 'EOF'
provider:
  type: slurm
  host: slurm-login.example.com
  port: 22
EOF
```
Full documentation: https://interlink-hq.github.io/interLink/"

-        "description": "Create a ModelConfig resource to connect to an LLM provider:
```bash

apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha1
kind: ModelConfig
metadata:
  name: openai-gpt4
spec:
  provider: openai
  model: gpt-4
  apiKeySecretRef:
    name: openai-key
    key: api-key
EOF
```
Create the secret first:
```bash
kubectl create secret generic openai-key --from-literal=api-key=<your-key> -n kagent
```"
+        "title": "Install kagent CRDs",

kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: k8s-helper
spec:
  systemPrompt: "You are a Kubernetes expert assistant."
  modelConfigRef:
    name: openai-gpt4
EOF
```"
+        "title": "Install kagent with an LLM provider",
+        "description": "Install kagent with your preferred LLM provider:
```bash

  --set providers.default=anthropic \
  --set providers.anthropic.apiKey=your-anthropic-api-key

# Or with local Ollama
helm install kagent ./helm/kagent/ --namespace kagent \
  --set providers.default=ollama
```
See https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/introduction/installation for all options."

     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Install KubeEdge CloudCore via Helm",
-        "description": "Add the official KubeEdge Helm repository and install CloudCore on the cloud-side Kubernetes cluster:

```bash
helm repo add kubeedge https://kubeedge.io/charts
helm repo update
```

Install CloudCore in the kubeedge namespace. Replace the advertise address with your cloud node's externally reachable IP:

```bash
helm install cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore \
  --namespace kubeedge \
  --create-namespace \
  --set cloudCore.modules.cloudHub.advertiseAddress[0]="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP"
```

**Alternative: Install using keadm CLI**

Download keadm and initialize the cloud side:

```bash
keadm init --advertise-address="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP" --kubeedge-version=v1.21.0
```"
+        "title": "Install keadm CLI",
+        "description": "Download the KubeEdge admin tool:
```bash
export KUBEEDGE_VERSION=v1.23.0
curl -Lo keadm.tar.gz https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/releases/download/${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}/keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar xzf keadm.tar.gz
sudo mv keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64/keadm/keadm /usr/local/bin/
rm -rf keadm.tar.gz keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify the CloudCore installation",
-        "description": "Check that CloudCore pods are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge
```

You should see the cloudcore pod in Running state."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Get the join token",
-        "description": "Retrieve the token needed for edge nodes to join the cluster:

```bash
keadm gettoken
```

Save this token for the next step."
+        "title": "Initialize the cloud side (control plane)",
+        "description": "On the cloud node (where kubectl has access):
```bash
keadm init --advertise-address=<cloud-node-ip> --kubeedge-version=${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}
```
This installs CloudCore on the cloud node."
       },
       {
         "title": "Join edge nodes",
-        "description": "On each edge node, run keadm join with the token from the cloud side. Cloud and edge versions must match:

```bash
keadm join --cloudcore-ipport="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP:10000" \
  --token=<TOKEN> \
  --kubeedge-version=v1.21.0
```"
+        "description": "Get the token from the cloud node:
```bash
keadm gettoken
```
On each edge node, join the cluster:
```bash
keadm join --cloudcore-ipport=<cloud-node-ip>:10000 --token=<token> --kubeedge-version=${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify edge nodes",
-        "description": "Check that the edge nodes have joined the cluster:

```bash
kubectl get nodes
```

Edge nodes will appear with the role 'edge'."

keadm version
```
Edge nodes should appear in the node list."

     ],
     "uninstall": [
       {
         "title": "Remove edge nodes",
-        "description": "On each edge node, reset the edge side:

```bash

-      },

-        "title": "Clean up CRDs and namespace",

```bash

```bash

```"

     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
-        "title": "CloudCore pod not starting",
-        "description": "Check the CloudCore pod logs for errors:

```bash

-      "summary": "A successful installation of KubeEdge will have CloudCore running in the kubeedge namespace on the cloud side, and EdgeCore running on each edge node. Edge nodes will appear in kubectl get nodes with the 'edge' role.",
+      "summary": "KubeEdge is installed with CloudCore on the cloud side and EdgeCore on edge nodes.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add kubeedge https://kubeedge.io/charts",
-        "helm install cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore --namespace kubeedge --create-namespace --set cloudCore.modules.cloudHub.advertiseAddress[0]="YOUR-CLOUD-NODE-IP"",
-        "kubectl get pods -n kubeedge"
+        "keadm init --advertise-address=<cloud-node-ip>",
+        "keadm join --cloudcore-ipport=<cloud-node-ip>:10000 --token=<token>"
       ]
     }
   },

-        "title": "Verify the installation",

-        "description": "Next, remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation:\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd pipelineruns.pipecd.dev\nkubectl delete crd applications.pipecd.dev\nkubectl delete crd environments.pipecd.dev\nkubectl delete crd deployments.pipecd.dev\n```"
-      },

-      {

+        "title": "Pull latest and upgrade",
+        "description": "```bash\ncd pipecd && git pull\nhelm upgrade pipecd manifests/pipecd --namespace pipecd\n```"
